# services.weblate

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.weblate`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.weblate.configurePostgresql` | `boolean` | Whether to enable and configure a local PostgreSQL server by creating a user and database for weblate. The default `settings` reference this database, if you disable this option you must provide a database URL in `extraConfig`. |
| `services.weblate.djangoSecretKeyFile` | `absolute path` | Location of the Django secret key. This should be a path pointing to a file with secure permissions (not /nix/store). Can be generated with `weblate-generate-secret-key` which is available as the `weblate` user. |
| `services.weblate.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Weblate service. |
| `services.weblate.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Text to append to `settings.py` Weblate configuration file. |
| `services.weblate.localDomain` | `string` | The domain name serving your Weblate instance. |
| `services.weblate.package` | `package` | The weblate package to use. |
| `services.weblate.smtp.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Weblate SMTP support. |
| `services.weblate.smtp.from` | `string` | The from address being used in sent emails. |
| `services.weblate.smtp.host` | `string` | SMTP host used when sending emails to users. |
| `services.weblate.smtp.passwordFile` | `null or absolute path` | Location of a file containing the SMTP password. This should be a path pointing to a file with secure permissions (not /nix/store). |
| `services.weblate.smtp.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | SMTP port used when sending emails to users. |
| `services.weblate.smtp.user` | `null or string` | SMTP login name. |
